digest.headline: Kimi K3 crashes the frontier, and the agent harnesses all shipped in the same week
digest.summary: Moonshot's 2.8T open model K3 took the top coding-leaderboard spot with free weights landing July 27, while Grok 4.5, Sonnet 5, GPT-5.6 and Gemini 3.1 keep the paid frontier crowded. Meanwhile Claude Code, Codex and Pi all pushed notable releases.

stories.title: Moonshot's Kimi K3 tops a major coding leaderboard — free weights drop July 27
stories.dek: The 2.8-trillion-parameter open model stunned the US industry over the weekend. Scale plus benchmark position plus open weights is the combination that makes this land differently from prior Chinese releases.
stories.whyItMatters: An open model at the top of the coding board resets what 'free/local' can do.

stories.title: xAI ships Grok 4.5, pitching ~2× token efficiency for agentic work
stories.dek: Released July 8 as xAI's new frontier model for coding, agents and professional knowledge work, with token efficiency framed as the headline advantage over the prior baseline.
stories.whyItMatters: Token efficiency is the new battleground for agent cost, not just raw benchmark scores.

stories.title: Claude Sonnet 5 becomes the default for all Free and Pro users
stories.dek: Launched June 30 with a 63.2% SWE-bench Pro score and $2/$10 intro pricing (rising to $3/$15 in September).
stories.whyItMatters: The default model most people touch just got materially stronger at coding.

stories.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 line (Sol, Terra, Luna) lands on the API with 1M context
stories.dek: A three-track line launched June 26, each with standard and Pro tiers, behind a managed access list. All share a 1M-token context window.
stories.whyItMatters: Three specialized tracks signal the shift away from one-model-fits-all.

stories.title: EU orders Google to open Android to AI rivals as Gemini slips again
stories.dek: Regulators push Google to admit competing assistants on Android, adding pressure during a rocky stretch for Gemini's rollout.
stories.whyItMatters: Distribution, not just model quality, may decide which assistant people actually use.

stories.title: Google opens an Applied AI Lab in Accra for African researchers
stories.dek: The Accra-based program gives African researchers and entrepreneurs early access to Google's AI and direct technical guidance.
stories.whyItMatters: Frontier tooling is starting to reach builders far outside the usual hubs.

modelsMeta.note: Best score per column is highlighted. DeepSWE's wide spread — Gemini 3.1 Pro at 12 vs GPT-5.6 Sol at 72.7 — is what separates models that SWE-bench had bunched together.

benchmarks.label: AA
benchmarks.full: Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index — composite of 9 evals

benchmarks.label: Cursor
benchmarks.full: CursorBench — agentic, repo-aware coding on real Cursor sessions

benchmarks.label: DeepSWE
benchmarks.full: DeepSWE — 113 long-horizon SWE tasks across 91 repos (Datacurve)

harness.notes: /fork now copies a conversation into a fresh background session while your current work continues. Plus filesystem-isolation controls, faster long sessions, and live MCP connector data for published artifacts.
Goal Mode points Codex at a long-horizon objective ("hours or even days") without manual steering. Codex Remote hits GA — start/approve work from the ChatGPT mobile app. TTFT down ~50%.
Adds Kimi K3 support and deferred tool loading (progressive extension activation). Pi has moved to its long-term home under the Earendil Works org; packages now publish under @earendil-works.

extensions.name: MCP spec 2026-07-28
extensions.blurb: Release candidate lands a stateless protocol core, an Extensions framework, Tasks, and MCP Apps. Final spec ships July 28.

extensions.name: Context7 Memory Manager
extensions.blurb: The most popular server on FastMCP right now — drop-in long-term memory for your agent.

extensions.name: Cloudflare MCP
extensions.blurb: Covers ~2,500 endpoints. The registry crossed 19,831 servers on Glama this spring — the ecosystem is exploding.

tips.title: Keep a 'source of truth' file in your repo root
tips.body: A CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / .cursorrules with your standards and naming conventions gets read first every session — so the agent writes code that looks like yours without re-explaining.

tips.title: Structure every prompt: Role + Task + Constraints + Output format
tips.body: Name the stack (e.g. Python 3.12 + FastAPI), paste the relevant code, and ask for tests. The structure beats cleverness.

tips.title: Always give the agent a check it can run
tips.body: Tests, a build, or a screenshot to compare. Without verification an agent stops the moment work merely 'looks done' — managing the task graph is the real 2026 skill.
